rationale: The citing paper uses Huang, Huang, and Jiang (2018) for more than a passing mention. It cites the paper in theorizing that existential/death-related threat cues can shape consumer behavior, and it explicitly borrows a death-thought measure from that paper in Study 3 to test alternative mechanisms. This gives the cited work a concrete conceptual and measurement role in the citing paper's design, even though it is not the main theory being extended.
relation_description: The citing paper applies insights and measurement elements from Huang et al. (2018) to a different context: community responses to fatal school shootings. It uses that prior mortality-salience stream to frame mechanism tests and operationalize death-thoughts, rather than directly refining or challenging Huang et al.'s conclusions.
